title: Bus and coach drivers
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of material-moving machine and vehicle operators and helpers. Supervise and coordinate activities of passenger attendants. All first-line supervisors of transportation workers not listed separately. (Based on 7 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect vehicles, check gas, oil, and water levels, and test vehicle equipment prior to departure to ensure proper operation and safety.
Comply with traffic regulations to operate vehicles in a safe and courteous manner, ensuring passenger safety and smooth rides.
Assist passengers, including elderly or disabled individuals, with boarding, seating, and luggage, and provide information about bus schedules or routes.
Handle passenger emergencies or disruptions and maintain order among passengers during trips to ensure safety.
Record information such as cash receipts, ticket fares, and maintain log books, along with submitting reports on trips and operational activities.
Collect tickets or cash fares from passengers and advise them on seating and conduct while on the vehicle.
Drive vehicles along specified routes or to designated destinations according to time schedules, ensuring compliance with traffic regulations.
Drive and handle various types of passenger vehicles, including those with specialized equipment, such as wheelchair lifts for passengers with special needs.

Maintain cleanliness of the vehicle interiors and exteriors, including vacuuming and washing the bus or motor coach, and perform routine maintenance such as regulating tire pressure and adding fuel or fluids.
Announce stops to passengers and report any mechanical issues or required repairs to supervisors or dispatchers using communications equipment.
Report delays, accidents, or emergencies, ensuring that all incidents are communicated effectively using the proper channels.
Attend safety meetings to keep updated on driver and passenger safety practices and make necessary adjustments to operational procedures accordingly.
Prepare and submit reports detailing work activities, including passenger numbers or trips, hours worked, mileage, or fuel consumption.
Pick up and drop off passengers at designated locations, following strict time schedules and safety regulations while ensuring passenger comfort.
Regulate vehicle comfort systems including heating, lighting, and ventilation for passengers during rides.
Provide assistance to passengers entering and exiting vehicles and ensure a safe boarding process, including helping small children cross roads when necessary.
Maintain knowledge of first-aid procedures to ensure passenger safety in emergencies.
Use maps and direction systems to effectively plan and navigate bus routes, following both written and verbal guidance.
Monitor for potential hazards during driving, including observing light signals and nearby traffic to ensure safe operations.
Engage with passengers, providing customer service feedback and resolving any inquiries related to travel arrangements or schedules.

detailed_work_activities.title: Provide transportation information to passengers or customers.
Notify others of emergencies, problems, or hazards.
Follow safety procedures for vehicle operation.
Assist customers to ensure comfort or safety.
Maintain vehicles in good working condition.
Receive information or instructions for performing work assignments.
Collect fares or payment from customers.
Monitor student behavior, social development, or health.
Measure the level or depth of water or other liquids.
Provide customers with general information or assistance.
Monitor surroundings to detect potential hazards.
Communicate with others to coordinate vehicle movement.
Direct material handling or moving activities.
Recommend personnel decisions or human resources activities.
Review work orders or schedules to determine operations or procedures.
Monitor work environment to ensure safety or adherence to specifications.
Inspect facilities to ensure compliance with safety, quality, or service standards.
Weigh materials to ensure compliance with specifications.
Direct passenger or freight transport activities.
Inspect facilities, equipment or supplies to ensure conformance to standards.
Inspect equipment to ensure proper functioning.
Support the professional development of others.
